reasoning: The market probability was around 34% as recently as Feb 20, 2025. Despite clear and growing tensions between Elon Musk and Trump's White House staff in Jan/Feb 2025 over federal employee ultimatums, immigration policies, and AI, Trump and Musk reportedly presented a 'united front' as of late February. There are many reports of internal annoyance (Trump joking he 'can't get Musk out of here', and general frustration with Musk's 'ubiquitousness'), but so far, Trump has publicly restrained himself from crossing the threshold of 'public disparagement' (e.g., belittling nicknames or 'never liked him'). Given Trump's high historical turnover rate and tendency to eventually turn on close advisors, alongside the high-pressure environment of Musk's White House involvement, the probability of an eventual public rebuke before Nov 2025 is substantial. However, considering Musk's immense structural power, wealth, and Trump's tendency to tolerate sycophantic billionaires longer than most staff, an outright public attack is far from guaranteed over the remaining ~8 months. Upward adjustments reflect the very tangible friction occurring right now, bringing the probability higher than the 0.34 baseline.
